req.title: Read patient
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-002 defines two access-control partitions (assigned vs. unassigned patient), an audit side-effect on success, and a 403 on unauthorised access. EP covers the happy-path partition; NEG covers the unassigned partition; BVA targets the exact assignment boundary (a patient assigned to exactly one clinician, read by that clinician vs. a different one); STC verifies the state of the audit log after a successful read; DT addresses the multi-condition truth table (role=clinician × assignment=true/false). Together these five tests give full branch coverage of the requirement's decision space.

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200
preconditions: Clinician is assigned to that patient
actions: Authenticate as assigned clinician to obtain bearer token
trace: R-HLT-002: clinician reads patient in their care → resource returned
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200(
    assigned_clinician_token,
    assigned_patient_id,
    base_url,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {assigned_clinician_token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Patient"
    assert body["id"] == assigned_patient_id

tests.name: test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403
preconditions: Clinician is NOT assigned to that patient
actions: Authenticate as unassigned clinician to obtain bearer token
expected: Response body contains FHIR OperationOutcome with issue[0].code == 'forbidden'
trace: R-HLT-002: error pathway – 403 when clinician not assigned
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403(
    unassigned_clinician_token,
    other_patient_id,
    base_url,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {unassigned_clinician_token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{other_patient_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("code") == "forbidden" for i in issues)

tests.name: test_clinician_reassigned_then_reads_patient_at_assignment_boundary
preconditions: Clinician A is initially NOT assigned to the patient (access denied state)
Clinician A is then assigned to exactly that one patient (minimum assignment cardinality = 1)
actions: Attempt GET /Patient/<boundary_patient_id> as Clinician A before assignment → expect 403
Assign Clinician A to <boundary_patient_id> via admin fixture
Attempt GET /Patient/<boundary_patient_id> as Clinician A after assignment → expect 200
expected: After assignment, second request returns HTTP 200
trace: R-HLT-002: boundary at assignment cardinality=1; transition from unassigned to assigned
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_clinician_reassigned_then_reads_patient_at_assignment_boundary(
    boundary_clinician_token,
    boundary_patient_id,
    assign_clinician_to_patient,
    base_url,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {boundary_clinician_token}"}
    url = f"{base_url}/Patient/{boundary_patient_id}"

    pre = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert pre.status_code == 403

    assign_clinician_to_patient(boundary_patient_id)

    post = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert post.status_code == 200
    assert post.json()["id"] == boundary_patient_id

tests.name: test_successful_read_writes_access_audit_entry
preconditions: Clinician is assigned to that patient
Audit log is accessible via admin endpoint /AuditEvent
actions: Query audit log for AuditEvent records matching this read
trace: R-HLT-002: expected outcome – access audit written after successful read
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_successful_read_writes_access_audit_entry(
    assigned_clinician_token,
    audit_patient_id,
    admin_token,
    base_url,
):
    hdrs = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {assigned_clinician_token}"}
    read_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{audit_patient_id}", headers=hdrs
    )
    assert read_resp.status_code == 200

    audit_hdrs = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Patient/{audit_patient_id}", "action": "R"},
        headers=audit_hdrs,
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1
    latest = entries[-1]["resource"]
    assert latest.get("action") == "R"
    assert latest.get("outcome") == "0"

tests.name: test_decision_table_role_and_assignment_combinations
preconditions: Clinician user NOT assigned to <dt_patient_id> exists
trace: R-HLT-002: decision table – (role × assignment) truth table fully exercised
